fresh_facts.claim: A third US aircraft carrier group has been deployed to the Middle East, marking the first time in 23 years that three carriers have operated in the same theater.
The US has implemented a global blockade targeting Iranian ports and vessels, with enforcement extending to the Indo-Pacific and seizures of Iranian-linked ships.
President Trump stated during a news conference on April 24-25 that the US has an 'ironclad blockade' from the Gulf of Oman to open oceans and is not anxious for a deal.
IRGC Quds Force chief Esmail Qaani issued a warning on April 24 that Iran and its 'resistance front' allies would make any aggressor regret launching war.
The IRGC Navy seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, as shown on state TV.
On April 24, explosive drones struck two Kuwaiti northern border posts, launched from Iraq by suspected Iran-linked militias, marking the first such attack on a US ally since the US-Iran ceasefire.
Kuwait condemned the drone strike on its border posts as 'sinful and aggressive,' viewing it as a message to Washington amid US financial pressure on Tehran.
Reports indicate Iran has granted commanders greater autonomy over Iraqi militias, with unverified footage suggesting pro-Iran Iraqi fighters deployed in Tehran streets.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed on April 25 that the US blockade is 'tightening by the hour' with 'nothing in, nothing out.'
A Fox News poll on April 25 shows 45% of Americans support military action against Iran.
Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, head of Iran's negotiating team, has stepped down amid internal disagreements over negotiations strategy.

Internal divisions exist within Iranian leadership, with civilian officials such as the foreign minister and parliament speaker favoring continued ceasefire and talks, while the Revolutionary Guard command opposes negotiations.
The IRGC reimposed its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz on April 18, 2026, one day after Iran announced reopening the critical waterway.

According to the Institute for the Study of War, the Iranian regime has conducted an 'unprecedented brutal crackdown' on recent protests, which marked 'the most serious domestic unrest that the Islamic Republic has ever faced.'
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: The Iranian regime arrested 'dozens' of security forces in Kermanshah who refused to fire at protesters.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: The IRGC stated it is 'dealing with possible acts of abandonment,' suggesting possible defections among security forces.
Nearly 5,000 Iranian-backed militia fighters from Iraq entered Iran to support the regime's crackdown on protests.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei ordered the Supreme National Security Council on January 9 to crush protests 'by any means necessary,' with orders to shoot to kill and 'show no mercy.'
fresh_facts.source: Iranian government/Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Iran's political stability index declined to -1.58 points in 2024 from -1.27 in 2023.
Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran to allow Tehran time to prepare a 'unified proposal.'
US and Pakistani mediators were awaiting a response from Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who was expected to respond on Wednesday.
Trump canceled a planned Pakistan trip by US envoys, stating Tehran's initial offer was 'not good enough' before Iran sent an improved version within minutes of the cancellation.
US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were heading to Islamabad on April 25-26 for direct talks with Iran's foreign minister.
Iran's Foreign Ministry insists talks focus on sanctions and guarantees.
President Trump claimed on April 25 that Iran's military is 'totally defeated' and its leaders are 'fighting like cats.'

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an intensifying multi-front crisis with three US carrier groups implementing a comprehensive global blockade while the regime experiences unprecedented internal fracture between negotiation-seeking civilians and hardline military commanders resisting talks, compounded by massive domestic protests and security force defections.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On June 16, 2026, the IDF struck Iranian regime security institutions and the Shahran oil depot near Tehran, while Iran launched approximately 30 ballistic missiles targeting Haifa in Operation True Promise III with 92% interception rates, and Supreme Leader Khamenei appointed Maj. Gen. Amir Hatami as Artesh commander; concurrently, three US carrier groups implemented a global blockade with Trump administration declaring an 'ironclad' embargo and direct diplomatic negotiations underway via US envoys.
